h1. Security and Access Control

h2. Purpose

Ensure metadata access is controlled before any retrieved context is passed to the LLM.

h2. Core Rule

{quote}
Security filtering must happen before context assembly and before LLM answer generation.
{quote}

h2. Access Dimensions

* User role
* Owner
* Node
* Business unit
* Lifecycle status
* Confidentiality classification
* PII/SPI flags
* Environment/source system

h2. Requirements

|| ID || Requirement || Priority ||
| SEC-001 | Define access-control model | P0 |
| SEC-002 | Apply filters to exact search | P0 |
| SEC-003 | Apply filters to semantic search | P0 |
| SEC-004 | Apply filters to graph search | P0 |
| SEC-005 | Prevent restricted chunks from entering LLM context | P0 |
| SEC-006 | Audit user queries and retrieved sources | P1 |
| SEC-007 | Build access-control test suite | P1 |
| SEC-008 | Define sensitive metadata handling | P1 |

h2. Success Criteria

* Unauthorized metadata is not retrieved.
* Deprecated records are excluded by default.
* Sensitive records are filtered based on policy.
* Query and retrieval decisions are auditable.
* Negative access tests pass.

h2. Pitfalls / Risks

|| Risk || Impact || Mitigation ||
| Filtering occurs after LLM context assembly | Data leakage | Filter before retrieval/context assembly |
| Sensitive metadata is over-logged | Compliance issue | Define logging redaction rules |
| Lifecycle status ignored | Stale/deprecated assets appear | Enforce lifecycle filters globally |
| Graph traversal bypasses filters | Data leakage via relationships | Apply filters to graph traversal and expansion |
